Administration.mission: To ensure seamless organisational operations through effective governance, documentation, and executive support that enables every department to function at peak efficiency.
goals: Maintain accurate corporate records and compliance documentation
Provide responsive executive and administrative support across all departments
Optimise office systems, workflows, and facilities management
Uphold corporate governance standards and regulatory compliance

Human Resources.description: People operations, talent acquisition and development
Human Resources.mission: To attract, develop, and retain exceptional talent while building a workplace culture where every employee is empowered to grow, contribute, and thrive.
goals: Source and onboard high-calibre candidates aligned with company values
Design and deliver impactful learning and development programmes
Ensure competitive, equitable compensation and benefits structures
Champion employee wellbeing, engagement, and retention initiatives

Finance.description: Financial planning, accounting, and treasury management
Finance.mission: To safeguard the financial integrity of ARCHER ENTERPRISE by delivering accurate reporting, strategic planning, and disciplined stewardship of all financial resources.
goals: Produce timely and accurate financial statements and management reports
Drive budget planning, forecasting, and variance analysis across all units
Ensure full compliance with US tax obligations and regulatory standards
Optimise cash flow, treasury operations, and cost management

Operations.description: Core business operations and service delivery
Operations.mission: To deliver consistent, high-quality service outcomes by continuously improving processes, logistics, and cross-functional coordination across the enterprise.
goals: Maintain service delivery standards that exceed client expectations
Identify and implement process improvements to reduce waste and costs
Coordinate logistics and supply chain operations efficiently
Build resilience through robust quality assurance and risk management

Technology.description: IT infrastructure, software engineering, and digital systems
Technology.mission: To power the digital backbone of ARCHER ENTERPRISE by building robust systems, securing data assets, and delivering technology solutions that accelerate business growth.
goals: Develop, maintain, and scale enterprise software and digital platforms
Ensure 99.9% uptime for critical infrastructure and internal systems
Protect company data and networks through proactive cybersecurity measures
Drive digital transformation initiatives across all business units

employees.purpose: Provides high-level administrative support to C-suite executives including calendar management, board preparation, correspondence handling, and stakeholder liaison. Acts as the primary point of coordination between executive leadership and all internal departments.

Oversees all day-to-day office functions including facilities management, vendor contracts, procurement of office supplies, and space planning. Responsible for maintaining a productive, compliant, and well-resourced work environment across all US office locations.

Manages the organisation's document lifecycle including filing, archiving, retrieval, and disposal of corporate records in line with US regulatory retention schedules. Supports compliance audits and maintains the integrity of the corporate document management system.

Ensures the company complies with US statutory and regulatory requirements. Prepares and maintains board meeting minutes, resolutions, and state filings. Advises leadership on governance obligations, Delaware corporate law, and shareholder communications.

Coordinates cross-departmental administrative activities including travel logistics, event planning, internal communications, and meeting scheduling. Serves as a central contact point for administrative requests from all US-based departments.

Serves as a strategic partner to department heads, aligning people practices with business objectives. Leads workforce planning, talent reviews, performance management cycles, and change management initiatives across assigned US business units.

Manages the full recruitment lifecycle from job briefing through to offer acceptance. Sources candidates via job boards, LinkedIn, and referrals; conducts competency-based screening; coordinates panel interviews; and ensures a positive candidate experience at every stage.

Designs, delivers, and evaluates enterprise-wide training programmes covering technical skills, leadership, onboarding, and compliance. Manages relationships with external training vendors and maintains the LMS to track employee development progress across all US offices.

Collects, analyses, and reports on HR metrics including headcount, attrition, time-to-hire, engagement scores, and absenteeism. Builds dashboards and periodic reports that support evidence-based people decisions by senior HR leadership.

Administers the company's US compensation framework including salary benchmarking, grading structures, and annual review processes. Manages employee benefits enrolment, health insurance liaison, 401(k) administration, and payroll inputs to ensure accurate and timely employee remuneration.

Leads the full accounting function including month-end and year-end close, preparation of financial statements under US GAAP, and primary liaison with external auditors. Oversees the finance team, sets internal controls policy, and presents financial performance reports to the CFO and board.

Supports the annual budget cycle, quarterly forecasting, and ongoing variance analysis against financial targets. Builds financial models to evaluate business cases, investment opportunities, and cost optimisation initiatives across all US departments.

Plans and executes internal audit assignments across finance, operations, and compliance functions. Assesses the adequacy of internal controls, identifies financial and operational risks, and delivers audit findings with actionable recommendations to management and the audit committee.

Coordinates the annual and supplementary budget preparation process across all departments. Monitors actual expenditure against approved budgets, flags variances, and works with department heads to ensure cost discipline and reforecast accuracy throughout the financial year.

Assists in monitoring daily cash positions, preparing bank reconciliations, and processing payments through the company's treasury management system. Supports foreign currency management, intercompany settlements, and liquidity reporting under the supervision of the Head of Finance.

Directs all operational activities across the US enterprise, setting service delivery standards, managing vendor relationships, and leading cross-functional project execution. Responsible for operational KPIs, SLA compliance, and continuous improvement of end-to-end business processes.

Manages the movement of goods, materials, and equipment across the US supply chain. Coordinates with vendors, freight carriers, and internal teams to ensure timely delivery, accurate inventory levels, and cost-effective logistics operations.

Identifies operational inefficiencies and designs improved workflows using Lean and Six Sigma methodologies. Leads process mapping workshops, develops standard operating procedures, and tracks the measurable impact of improvement initiatives on cost, speed, and quality.

Manages client-facing service delivery activities, ensuring contractual obligations are met and service levels consistently achieved. Acts as the primary escalation contact for operational issues, tracks service tickets to resolution, and prepares monthly service performance reports for clients.

Develops and enforces quality assurance standards across all operational processes. Conducts regular quality audits, manages non-conformance reports, and drives corrective action programmes. Ensures adherence to ISO standards and US regulatory quality management frameworks.

Designs, builds, and maintains enterprise web applications and internal tools. Leads technical architecture decisions, conducts code reviews, and mentors junior engineers. Responsible for delivering scalable, well-tested software that meets business requirements on schedule and within scope.

Manages the CI/CD pipeline, AWS cloud infrastructure, containerisation (Docker/Kubernetes), and deployment automation. Monitors system performance, manages incidents, and ensures reliable, secure, and cost-optimised infrastructure to support all digital products.

Transforms raw business data into actionable insights through SQL querying, data modelling, and BI dashboard development (Power BI, Tableau). Works with department heads to define KPIs, automate reporting, and support data-driven decision-making across the company.

Protects the company's digital assets by monitoring for threats, managing vulnerability assessments, and responding to security incidents. Develops and enforces information security policies, conducts employee security awareness training, and ensures compliance with SOC 2, NIST, and US data protection regulations.

Builds and maintains responsive user interfaces for internal tools and client-facing web applications using React and modern CSS frameworks. Collaborates with the senior engineer and product team to translate design mockups into pixel-perfect, accessible, and performant UI components.
